observations.recommendation: We recommended the following to the management: a. The Municipal Engineer shall provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects. b. The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. c. The OIC-Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card. He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We recommended that all cash advances granted be fully liquidated at year end through the submission of liquidation report or refund of the un-utilized balances and comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Also, cash advances should be accounted and reported separately for faster liquidation and recording of this account.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts, POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.
We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

We recommended management to ensure that all supporting documents pertaining to procurement are properly accomplished and corresponding regulations for each type of procurement are adhered to. Disbursements made contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.
We recommended management to ensure that all disbursements are compliant with Section 4 of PD 1445 and other pertinent rules and regulations otherwise it will be disallowed in audit, as warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of communication expenses to officials and employees; (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economic reasons and to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU; and (c) ensure that all disbursements are supported with the required documents to avoid suspension or possible disallowance in audit.

We strongly recommended that management hire consultants only for those works that require highly and specialized skills and expertise and for a specific project of the Municipal Government which are determined to be not within the capability of its regular employees to perform, and to comply strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and COA Circular No. 95-011. Also, we recommended that the agency furnishes the Office of the Auditor a copy of the bidding documents, and other pertinent papers as provided under RA 9184 and Sanggunian Bayan Resolution authorizing the LCE to enter into contract for the procurement of consultancy services.

We reiterated our recommendation to management to issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that they are in the process of conducting related research and study of the various essential inputs to come up with the desired policy guidelines for the agency.

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institutions.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

To correct the deficiencies noted, we recommended the following measures:

A logbook should be maintained by the issuing officials so that he could monitor and regulate the fuel/gasoline issuances.

Equipment log for the equipment’s of the LGU should be maintained so that daily consumption of diesel could be monitored.

Only the consumption of the municipality’s machineries and equipment should be recorded as gasoline expenses. Grants and donations to constituents in the form of gasoline should be recorded in the appropriate accounts.

All data in the trip ticket should be properly filled-up and it should observe strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 prescribing the use of the Manual on Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles to enable the Auditor to evaluate properly the fuel consumption of each motor vehicle and heavy equipment maintained by the municipality.

We recommended that management immediately terminate the legal consultancy services of the private lawyer and strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 98-002.  The services of the Provincial Legal Officer or the Provincial Prosecutor should be requested in case the municipality needs legal services consistent with Section 481 of RA 7160.

We recommended that management should hire consultants, if really needed, in accordance with RA 9184.  Specialization and expertise should be taken into consideration before hiring them and the actual need should be assessed to avoid duplication in functions. Stop hiring consultants who do not have highly technical expertise or whose services are within the capabilities of agency personnel. Also, accomplishment reports and DTRs or attendance logbooks of the consultants should be submitted to justify the payments made to them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should review the said account, secure the necessary supporting documents needed as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and submit a request for authority to write off from COA, in order to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.

We reiterated our recommendation that management should finish the conduct of actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386.  The inventory report prepared by the General Service Officer (GSO) should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of inventories.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that cash advances which remained unliquidated beyond the allowed time of liquidation be withheld from the salaries of the accountable officers and proper charges be made, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.
We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

We recommended that management settle the disallowances by withholding the payment of the salaries or any amount or cash due to the accountable/liable persons and request from COA for installment payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended that management require the Accountable Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as basis in the preparation of Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 2 and to ensure that all accountable forms received and issued are properly accounted for and prompt submission of said monthly report to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit is enjoined.

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.

The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that management implement more strict controls in the payment of wages and salaries by directing the Disbursing Officer thru the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments should only be made directly to the names appearing in the payroll, otherwise, a written authority to collect should be presented by the person claiming the payment.

We recommended the management to stop the practice of splitting of contracts and strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in all the procurements to prevent sanctions for violation of the procurement law.

We recommended management to ensure that PPAs to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund should be carefully planned and must partake the nature of capital expenditures. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer should discontinue the practice of charging prior year’s expenditure to current year appropriation and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation be observed. We recommended that management require the concerned DepEd employees and other accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. observations.recommendation: 1.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the liquidating officer to deposit all collections in order to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. 2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted. A request for authority to write-off the cash advances of deceased employees, separated from service and those whose whereabouts are no longer known should be prepared and submitted to COA as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.  Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.

3.4 We recommended that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the inventory committee should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

4.2 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.

6.3 We recommend that the management evaluate the status of project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by NGAs.  Savings from implemented projects should be returned to the source agency.

7.5 We recommended that management strictly monitor and fast track  the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents. We also recommend the municipal officials adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 of the DILG and DBM in the utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
8.4 We recommended that management stop charging  administrative/operating expenses under the 20% development fund.  Adherence to the provisions of  DILG and DBM Joint MC 2017-1 is highly enjoined.

9.3 We recommend that management require the concerned DepEd employees and other accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. No cash advance shall be granted unless the outstanding balances is liquidated by them. Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

10.4 We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to fully utilize the appropriated amount for solid waste management and environmental protection and comply with the completion and operation of a sanitary landfill for proper solid waste management.
11.2 We commend the management in implementing various PPAs which are supportive of GAD. We also recommend that the implementation of PPAs should be maximized to attain the purpose of GAD.

12.3 We recommended that management should facilitate the settlement of disallowed payments, and for the Disbursing Officer/cashier to withhold from the salaries of accountable officers should they fail to settle their disallowances despite the issuance of notice and demand. Also, Disallowances with Notice of Finality of Decision should be recorded in the books of the agency to properly recognize the receivables of the government.

13.1 We commended the management for its efforts in implementing programs and projects that protect children’s rights in the municipality with a recommendation that more programs which directly benefits and protects the children in the municipality will be implemented.

15.4 We recommend that management should ensure that vehicles of the municipal government are properly marked with “For Official Use Only” as prescribed under COA Circular No. 75-6, has the logo of the LGU placed in its sides and all trips made thereon are properly monitored with corresponding documentations.

16.4 We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to   avoid  sanctions   that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

17.3 We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

18.3 We recommended that management observe the limitations on the expenditures for costumes and other related expenses during the conduct of cultural and athletic activities. Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

19.4 We recommended that management implement more strict controls in the payment of wages and honorarium by directing the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments should only be made directly to the names appearing in the payroll, otherwise, a written authority to collect should be presented by the person claiming the payment.

20.7 We recommended that the BAC ensures that all procurements should be from bonafide suppliers/dealers and the GSO should furnish the BAC an updated list of bonafide suppliers/dealers of the Municipal Government.

21.6 We recommended that management ensure that all disbursements are supported with the required documents as stated under the above-stated regulations.  The required attendance sheet and the documentary requirements shall be submitted to avoid disallowance in audit.

The office in charge in making the remittances should ensure that all payments to GSIS and Pag-ibig are updated and deducted from the payroll to avoid over-remittances in the future.

24.6 We recommended that management stop making payments in advance and ensure that the money due to the government will be recovered. Adherence to rules and regulations on government procurement is enjoined.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in the classification of receipts made by the LGU. Draw the necessary journal entry voucher to reclassify Due to LGU’s and Due to NGA’s accounts of P3,514,519.44 to Prior Year’s Adjustments in order that the unrecorded income shall accrue to the Government Equity accounts.
observations.managementAction: Analysis of the affected accounts are in process with the view of reclassifying these accounts the appropriate accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management formulate policies and guidelines to be strictly adopted in the operations of the Economic Enterprise Units so as to enable the unit to generate income which shall be used to cover all the necessary expenses and make it a real self-liquidating enterprise. Otherwise, the units which fail to meet its targeted revenues and remain dependent on subsidies to cover its operations be dissolved.
observations.managementAction: The operations of the EEU of the LGU are indefinitely responded/abolished funding this to be invisible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management particularly the Municipal Treasurer  a) transfer/deposit in full the LDRRM Fund from the surplus of the general fund otherwise concerned officials are liable for technical malversation; and b) restore the funds of the LDDRMF that were irregularly disbursed for purposes other than it was provided for, and to ensure that the LDRRMF be exclusively disbursed for projects and activities that are related to disaster, relief and recovery programs and activities so as not to compromise the implementation of the projects or activities under the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and Appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Irrelevant charges were stopped and not incurred for the year 2016. However, the fund was still deficient and not transferred to the account maintained for its purpose.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no.7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officers be required to immediately liquidate/refund their outstanding cash advances under payroll fund, and for the Municipal Accountant to allow only withdrawals for payroll fund equivalent to the net of payroll and to be supported with approved vouchers for payment.
observations.managementAction: Withdrawals for payroll fund are now stopped. Management adopted the Automated Teller Machine in disbursing salaries for regular employees. Also, the unliquidated amount of P15.346 million have now decreased to P5.429 million.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 10 of this report.

observations.managementAction:
Cash overdraft had decreased from ₱80,420,017.40 to ₱32,664,429.03 through the effort undertaken by the Local Finance Committee (LFC).

Prompt remittance of trust collections to GSIS, BIR and other NGO’s was accelerated.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 11 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer/Property Officer maintain property ledger cards and reconcile the amount per actual count and the recorded balances in the general ledgers to come up with an accurate and reliable inventory report and establish the existence of P99,536,802.76 cost properties.
observations.managementAction: Report of inventory of LGU properties was still not submitted to the Auditor and no reconciliation of actual inventory and accounting records was ever made.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 18 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management expedite the titling of the sanitary landfill in the name of the Municipal Government of Nagtipunan, copy furnished the COA through the Audit Team assigned in the LGU of all the TCTs.
observations.managementAction: Titling of these lands is already on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the balances of the accounts affected be adjusted with the appropriate asset account and that depreciation of the assets be provided.
observations.managementAction: Reclassifications of these accounts were made by the Accounting Office. A JEV was drawn.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management properly allocate and utilize the fund in accordance with Section 272 of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The management has already complied with the proper utilization, allocation and transfer of the Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that management prioritize and maximize implementation of programs/projects geared towards environmental protection and services.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has implemented the priority projects identified under the MENRO.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has still not yet adopted any policy regarding the hiring of casual/contractual/job order employees. However, the number has already decreased from 421 to 288 personnel.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management immediately terminate the legal consultancy services of the private lawyer and strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 98-002.  We recommended that the management instead request the services of the Provincial Legal Officer or the Provincial Prosecutor in case the municipality needs legal services consistent with Section 481 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The municipality  hired a lawyer as a contractual employee which would deliver services according to the contract executed between the two parties.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the management:

A. Submit the liquidation reports of fund transfers from the national government agencies in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

B. Fast track the implementation of the fund transfers since the resources are readily available, and in order not to deprive the Municipality of the PPAs where the funds are intended for.

C. Immediately return to the Source Agency the unutilized amounts of projects funds that were fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The agency has already submitted partial liquidations of the funds downloaded from national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned responsible officers to strictly adhere with the requirements on the submission of accounts and financial reports pursuant to Paragraph No. 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 122 of PD 1445 and other existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management has partially complied with the submission of accounts. However, there are some vouchers which incurred delays from 5 to 10 months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enforce immediate settlement of all audit disallowances from the persons deemed liable, thru cash refund, to ensure recovery of the funds of the LGU that were disbursed irregularly.
observations.managementAction: Management collected partial refunds of audit disallowances. Audit disallowances with COE were collected thru payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Reiterated a finding no. 3 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The management has already drawn a journal entry voucher for the partial
reconciliation of the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management A) be more prudent in its expenditures and austerity measures be undertaken by management B) stop unnecessary expenditures and strict compliance with the above cited rules and regulation is enjoined c) refund the amount used to purchase for high-end cellular phones.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of high-end gadgets were already limited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop granting cash advances as a way of procuring goods and services needed by the entity. Limit the procurement of goods and services on reimbursement basis in small quantity and amount out of petty cash fund granted only, unless the circumstance warrant otherwise. Ensure that all procurement above the threshold stated under RA 9184 undergo public bidding.
observations.managementAction: The municipality had partially complied with the provisions of RA 9184 through adopting measures to limit procurement through reimbursement and cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that request for the procurement of medicines be supported with a certification of the requisitioning officer that the medicines fall within and conform to the PNDF Volume 

In addition, we recommended Management ensure that their supplier be the direct distributor to guarantee that the medicines are of low prices but not sacrificing its quality.
observations.managementAction: Strict observance to the audit recommendation was already directed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the job order employees to refund the amount claimed for traveling expenses shown in the attached schedule since this is prohibited. We also recommended that management stop the practice of allowing them to attend trainings and seminars, then entitling them to claim traveling expenses thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The municipality has already stop granting travelling expenses for seminars to job order employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

A. The Local Finance Committee make representation with the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the enactment of an ordinance appropriating an additional amount in the current budget for unpaid obligations not taken up as Accounts Payable. The Municipal Budget Officer should prepare listings during the year to support the request for an appropriation.
B. The Municipal Treasurer be vigilant and careful in certifying the cash availability in the disbursement vouchers. 
C. The Budget Officer to issue a monthly advice of allotment as an authority to incur obligations. Obligation Request (OBR) should only be prepared when the goods/services are delivered and rendered based on the approved Purchase Order in cases of purchases.
D. The Municipal Accountant reconcile with the Budget Office every end of the quarter or at the end of the year obligations versus actual payments to determined vouchers processed but not paid to be taken up as accounts payable.
observations.managementAction: Necessary expense accounts were already adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management exert extra-effort in locating the grantee of the outstanding loan receivable so that the loan repayment shall be collected, otherwise, a request for write-off be submitted to the COA for the long outstanding loan receivables.
observations.managementAction: The grantees of the loan were located and the negotiations were already in place for the settlement of the mount.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 36 of this report.
